meta.description: Canonical intent for scheduled work that touches AINL wrappers or related OpenClaw jobs. One row per logical job; execution_owner names who actually fires the clock.

execution_owners.openclaw: OpenClaw cron store (openclaw cron list). This is the usual execution truth for agentTurn jobs.
execution_owners.system: OS crontab, launchd, systemd timer, or Advocate daemon — host-owned.
execution_owners.none: AINL source declares S core cron for IR / emit / docs only; nothing external must fire on that expression unless you promote it.

meta.portability_note: Any OpenClaw user can adopt this pattern: ship your own cron_registry.json (or CRON_REGISTRY_PATH), define openclaw_match.payload_contains from YOUR job payloads, and set untracked_payload_substrings to strings that appear only in YOUR automation (or omit for no heuristic).

jobs.notes_for_agents: Do not add a second crontab entry for this if OpenClaw already runs it. To migrate system cron → OpenClaw: disable the system line, then openclaw cron add with the same runner_hint. To disable: openclaw cron disable (or UI) — do not delete AINL source; update this registry enabled field.
Same ownership rules as wrapper_supervisor. CRM health URL comes from frame crm_health_url / CRM_* env (see run_wrapper_ainl.py).
GitHub + CRM digest path; keep GITHUB_TOKEN and CRM_* aligned with adapters/openclaw_defaults.py defaults.
